title: Approximation
summary: Approximation represents values close enough for a defined purpose or tolerance.
claim: Approximation represents a value close enough to a target value for a defined purpose, tolerance, or measurement resolution.
proof: Repeated measurement shows that physical values often cannot be represented exactly; tolerances define when a value is useful enough for purpose.
validation: Level 5 as a formal concept; practical use depends on measurement context.
reconstruction: Can be rediscovered by measuring the same object repeatedly with limited tools.
